Tangled webs

March 2004
Investigative MethodsTechnology

IRE and NICAR are exploring social network analysis as a tool for journalists to visualize relationships between individuals, businesses, and institutions, a technique long used by law enforcement and other professionals. The Washington Post demonstrated its effectiveness in 2003 when investigating visa mill language schools.

IRE and NICAR are beginning to more deeply explore social network analysis for visualizing and diagramming relationships between individuals, businesses and institutions. It's a new approach for journalists, but business consultants, law enforcement agencies and public health investigators have used this approach for decades. In 2003, The Washington Post used social network analysis to report on a group of Los Angeles-area language schools that appeared to be "visa mills" for foreign students.

For more information and a list of Web links and tipsheets on this emerging technique, go to www.ire.org/sna.
